comment_text:
How can you call it live action if the majority of the show is CGI? 

I know they have sets ands whatnot and put work into some of the ships but they will cut corners sooner than later, if they haven’t already. 

With the massive undertaking that one piece it’ll only get goofier and more cringe than the original and The actors will quickly age out. 

I love the anime even with its cornucopia of flaws but it’s gotten tiring with a lot of the concept changes and new directions that don’t really mesh well with me personally. 

I’m happy to welcome more fans to the franchise but this feels like the cheap and Disney way of doing things. No legitimacy in this and it’s just kind of sad.

video_title: ONE PIECE: Season 2 | Chopper First Look | Netflix
comment_text:
This gives me a lot of hope for season 2. Drum island is one of my favorite early grand line arcs, mainly because it introduces chopper who is one of my favorite straw hats. I admit to being somewhat biased due to liking chopper so much, if they had really messed up his design, much less his character, which they also got right, i probably would've lost interest in the show altogether.

Also, apparently some people dislike this design, though thankfully those people are in the vast minority as i have heard heaps of positivity regarding live action chopper. Honestly, if someone like me who absolutely adores the original chopper likes this design, the "haters" have no excuse.

I'm looking forward to season 2 even more now that i've seen this and the official trailer, from what i've seen so far it's all looking good. So long as they keep the "for the fans" mentality up, we could get something truly great.

comment_text:
2026? AH come on. Is at least half a year until then. Well they did a nice work on Chopper so I cant wait for his transformation. Also I can finisih watching the anime while waiting. I thought they were really skipping ahead but Tony Tony Chopper first appears in One Piece episode 78, which is part of the Drum Island arc. So approx 44 episodes due to the length with intro and outro 25 min also lots of fillers and compare to manga its 95 chapters. So the queeze is kinda fitting. Something is missing however when the on that restaurant boat cause what happend to the big match? Also the mermaid was not there what I can remember. This is what happens and yes is long before episode 78.

The Baratie Arc is the fourth story arc of the manga and anime One Piece, and the fourth story arc of the East Blue Saga.

The Straw Hat Pirates meet Johnny and Yosaku, old friends of Zoro, who direct them to the restaurant ship Baratie, a "floating oasis" in the middle of the ocean. There they meet chef Sanji, who Luffy wishes to recruit as his crew's cook. However, pirate admiral Don Krieg sets his sights on the Baratie as a replacement for his devastated fleet, and the restaurant soon comes under siege. As the battle breaks out, Zoro comes face to face with his ultimate rival, Dracule Mihawk, while Nami absconds with the Going Merry, forcing the crew to split up and take chase while Luffy protects the Baratie from Krieg.

Now I see what they did here Don Krieg is the serie but attack from Mihawk would leave your life hanging on a thread still this happened in the manga as well and be desperate move Don Krieg attacked Luffy and other on The Baratie. Still think it was a bad move to replace that battle with the fishfolks. If the casting would be difficult the very same actors for the fishfolk could just jump off that suit and be the surving crew of Don Krieg and give that actor more role to play then just barel 5 min on stage. Is not like the fishfolk gonna have plenty of story later. Well still watchable still great awesome staff etc.

comment_text: 2026... That's the thing bothering me the most with that One Piece live action. It's amazing, but I fear they're relying way too much on "quality" instead of "quantity", which, depending on the point of view, is actually quantity versus quality. It's true we love polished, passionate products, but the point of a love-action is to have actors acting, scene adapters writing, and preferably more than once every three years then sitting while they wait for the visuals effects to be made. It's good to have CGI, but we don't actually care THAT much, because again, it's not the experience we're looking for. Most people would gladly trade a whole year or two of visual editing if we could get twice more seasons before that whole cast of "teens" turn in their literal 30s years old. We need a quantity of quality acting, not visual effects we could see anywhere else nowadays. The alternate route is to make them all eternally young in CGI, like Chopper... and well, why not rendering them in anime-style as we're at it ?
